# Publication themes {.unnumbered}

A figure is not done when it looks right on your screen. It has to land in a
journal at the right size, on a poster legible from across a hall, or on a slide
that reads from the back of the room. Each medium has its own demands -- panel
dimensions, text size, file format -- and the same plot needs a different finish
for each.

This part covers getting a figure out the door. The manuscript chapter handles
fixed-panel sizing and PDF export for submission, plus editable PowerPoint output
when a figure needs to keep changing. The research-day chapter handles poster
sizing, where the base text has to be large enough to read at a distance. The
house themes do most of the work; these chapters show how to pair them with the
right save settings so the figure arrives the way you drew it.
